title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Docker Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares fresh buildx builders, multi-platform images, bake, and oci outputs decisions and accepts only answers tied to observable image, container, build, or Compose evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers
steps.instruction:
Order the Docker Buildx Builders Drivers container delivery evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Docker Buildx Builders Drivers assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, keep Docker Buildx Builders Drivers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.text: A quiz release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Docker Buildx Builders Drivers has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Buildx Builders Drivers precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A quiz release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.

For Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Every Buildx builder shares the daemon image store, cache, platforms, and outputs.

options.text: Capture Docker versions, active context, desired artifact, current state, authority, and immutable identities for the quiz Docker Buildx Builders Drivers case
